name: Cats Tongues
recipeIngredient: 3 large Egg whites; or 2 large Eggs
recipeInstructions:
Preheat the oven to 400°F. Brush 3 or 4 large, heavy baking sheets with melted butter. Dust with flour. Place the butter in a small stainless steel bowl and beat with a wooden spatula, warming it over low heat as needed to make it smooth, white, and creamy. Sift the sugar over the butter and beat it in. Beat in 1 egg or egg white with the wooden spatula. Beat in the remaining eggs or whites one at a time with a wire whisk. Whisk in the vanilla. Sift the flour over the batter and mix it in with the wooden spatula.
Scoop the batter into a large pastry bag fitted with a 5/16-inch plain nozzle (Ateco #3) and pipe the batter on the diagonal, making fingers that are 2-1/2 inches long and 3/8 inch wide at the center. Arrange them on the baking sheets in staggered rows and separate them by 1-1/2 inches. The ends of each finger should be fatter than the center, like a dumbbell. Bake, one sheet at a time, until the cookies are lightly browned around the edges but still pale in the center, about 6 to 10 minutes. Slide the cookies onto a wire rack using a metal spatula and let cool completely before storing them in a covered airtight container for up to 1 week.
